disjointness: calibration 80e6 & validation 90e6 disjoint from each other, from P3(20/50/60/70e6), and > forbidden max 14100047
router_rule: ALLOW if B3_method_confirm(b3_state==CONCEPT_CONFIRMED) AND observed_T>=tau ; else ABSTAIN (monotone: allow-set is a strict subset of B3 confirms)
grid: deterministic: sorted unique observed_T among CALIBRATION B3-method-confirms + sentinels {0, +inf}
tau_lock_rule: tau_R1 = smallest grid tau s.t. NULL_cov allow<=3/300 AND NULL_cov_plus_label allow<=3/300 (conservative). Also report tau_boundary(<=7/300) and tau_zero(0/300). Lock tau_R1 (NOT the exposed R0 tau).
calibration_null_only: POS conditions NOT run or inspected in calibration before tau lock

validation_endpoints.primary_safety: NULL_cov allow<=7/300 AND NULL_cov_plus_label allow<=7/300 (cap kept at 7 to avoid boundary; do NOT rely on CP borderline)
validation_endpoints.secondary_safety: NULL_label<=3/300 preferred, random_label_control<=3/300 preferred; R1 SAFETY-FAIL if either secondary >7/300

invalid_accounting: method-invalid => ABSTAIN; denominator=300 per condition; n_invalid reported explicitly; invalid NOT removed from denominator unless implementation failure
no_retune: if held-out primary safety fails, do NOT retune tau on validation -- that IS the result
worker: realeeg_r1_forensic.py (reuses the validated P3 realeeg_internal_forensic.run_cohort = certify_paired_calibrated; B3 method path UNCHANGED)
